Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Terraform Digital and Rachel Nguyen using search_emails (query: "Terraform" or "Rachel Nguyen" or "Vantage Distributed"). Read relevant threads using get_email_by_id or get_thread. Discovery: (a) Rachel Nguyen (rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com, VP of People Operations) sent the first relocation batch request to Julian (email-terraform-reloc-request-001), listing 3 employees: Priya Dasgupta (Sr. Data Engineer, Austin TX → Chicago IL, move-in first week of May / May 5), Marcus Liu (Product Designer, Portland OR → New York NY, move-in around May 12), and Samantha Obi (DevOps Engineer, Denver CO → San Francisco CA, move-in May 8, must be at SF office by May 11). (b) Julian forwarded to David Chen with Rachel CC'd, calling Rachel "my primary contact" (email-terraform-reloc-fwd-001). (c) Internal pricing discussion followed among David, Marcus Thorne, and Alejandro about margin floor and tiered discounts. (d) Catalina Dubois accidentally Reply-All'd the internal pricing thread to Rachel (email-terraform-replyall-001), exposing MoveOps' 25% margin floor and tiered discount structure. (e) Rachel acknowledged the leak professionally but firmly, noted Julian had been her "only point of contact since we signed" (email-terraform-rachel-response-001). (f) Catalina sent an apology email confirming Elena assigned Mina Hashimoto as dedicated AM and Julian would continue to be available (email-terraform-apology-001). (g) Mina sent introduction email to Rachel restating the 3 relocations, positioning herself as dedicated AM for relocation services (email-terraform-mina-intro-001). (h) Rachel replied to Mina with detailed intake information for the employees including Priya's cat requiring pet-friendly housing (email-terraform-rachel-details-001), with Julian CC'd.
OE 2: Search Slack for Terraform and Vantage Distributed discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Vantage Distributed" or "Vantage"). Read relevant threads using conversations_replies. Discovery: (a) Julian announced the Vantage Distributed deal in #customer-engagement (C002), $1,850,000, 1,000+ employees. (b) Catalina set up portal admin accounts for Rachel Nguyen, Derek Liu, and Sarah Chen. (c) Julian tied the AWS cost spike to the Vantage Distributed onboarding traffic in #root-cause-aws-spike (C009), admitting he should have advocated more for infrastructure cost review before finalizing pricing. (d) All three AMs declined Terraform account ownership, Mina cited Vectral crisis, Catalina cited NorthWind, Emeka cited Mosaic damage claim, before Elena assigned to Mina. (e) Julian later stated in #customer-engagement: "Rachel sent her request to me because I'm the person she trusts" and expressed regret about not staying as POC.
OE 3: Search CRM for Terraform Digital / Vantage Distributed using crm_search_companies (name: "Vantage Distributed") or crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform"). Find company record (company-terraform-digital, name: "Vantage Distributed", domain: vantagedistributed.com, 1,200 employees). Then search for deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company-terraform-digital"). Discovery: (a) deal-terraform-digital-001: "Vantage Distributed - Enterprise Relocation Package", $1,850,000, closedWon, closed 2026-03-18. (b) deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1: "Terraform Digital, Q2 2026 Relocation Batch 1 (3 employees)", $7,500, qualification stage, created 2026-04-23. Description lists Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi with routes and May dates, AM: Mina Hashimoto, dedicated coordinator authorized. Then check engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]). Discovery: engagement-terraform-replyall-incident documenting the full Reply-All incident timeline, exposed pricing details, resolution actions, and current status ("Relationship stabilized. Rachel is engaged and providing relocation details. Monitoring closely").
OE 4: Search Airtable for Terraform relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Terraform"). Also try value: "Vantage". Discovery: NO relocation records exist in the Relocations table (tblRelocations01) for any of the 3 Terraform employees. The relocations have not been set up. A separate search of the Client Accounts table (tblClientAccts01) finds recTerraformDigital01 showing Client: "Terraform Digital", Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto, Status: Onboarding, Total Relocations: 3, Notes documenting the Reply-All incident and that "Previous account contact: Julian Brooks... Mina is AM of record." This confirms the relocations are NOT on track, no coordinator assigned, no vendor contacts, no housing or flights arranged, and Priya Dasgupta's May 5 move-in is only 9 days away from the universe date of April 26.
OE 5: Search emails and Slack for AWS infrastructure resolution using search_emails (query: "AWS" or "cost spike" or "infrastructure") and/or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "AWS" or "cost spike", filter_in_channel: "C009"). Discovery: Samira Tariq published a formal RCA identifying the root cause as Vantage Distributed onboarding traffic, self-serve portal hit by ~1,200 users caused autoscaling from 4 to 18 instances, 3x cost increase to approximately $42,870 vs. $14,300 baseline. Resolution: CloudWatch alarms being implemented, pricing model adjustments underway, Elena directed the team to add cost alerts. The infrastructure concern has been identified and addressed with an RCA, but the agent should note whether cost protections are fully deployed for the upcoming relocation wave.
OE 6: Search emails for ExpenseBot / expense auto-categorizer pilot using search_emails (query: "expense auto-categorizer" or "pilot" or "auto-approve" or "auto-reject"). Read relevant threads using get_email_by_id or get_thread. Discovery: (a) Samira Tariq launched the auto-categorizer pilot with Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics as pilot clients. (b) Vectral experienced false rejections: legitimate expenses rejected due to stale default policy limits (e.g., ergonomic cap showed $400 instead of correct $600, internet cap showed $100 instead of $150). Mina escalated after Rachel Whitfield complained about Danielle Osei's $475 chair rejection. (c) Mosaic experienced false approvals: out-of-policy expenses approved due to malformed JSON config, excluded_categories stored as string instead of array (exclusion checks skipped), max_amount stored as string causing broken comparisons. Emeka reported Andre Washington's $1,200 desk (over $800 cap) and $350/month coworking (excluded category) approved in error, approximately $1,550 exposure. (d) Elena issued a pause directive (email_elena_directive): suspend auto-approve and auto-deny, keep auto-categorize with human review, order full audit. (e) David Chen escalated client relationship impact citing Vectral trust erosion and Mosaic reconciliation risk. (f) Marcus Thorne raised financial concerns and requested full audit halt on auto-approvals.
OE 7: Search emails for duplicate receipt and stipend fraud findings using search_emails (query: "duplicate receipt" or "stipend audit" or "TechComfort" or "SHA-256"). Discovery: Dmitri Volkov emailed Marcus Thorne (email_dmitri_receipt_audit_findings, subject: "Stipend audit finding, duplicate receipt anomalies across 3 clients (Vectral, Mosaic, BrightLoop)") with three flagged transactions: (1) Kevin Tran (Vectral Systems), $89.00 ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, STIP-2026-0089, already PAID on 2026-03-08. (2) Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics), $89.00 identical keyboard from TechComfort Supply, STIP-2026-0094, Pending, byte-for-byte identical SHA-256 hash to Tran's receipt. (3) Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop Analytics), $45.00 monitor stand from TechComfort Supply, STIP-2026-0097, Pending, different hash but same PDF template metadata (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). Total exposure: $223.00 ($89 paid + $134 pending). Marcus replied (email_marcus_qb_status_reply) confirming he placed administrative holds on both pending payments (STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097). Note: BrightLoop Analytics was not one of the two pilot clients (Vectral and Mosaic), so the duplicate receipt issue spans beyond the pilot scope.
OE 8: Search emails for AM communications to HR contacts about receipt verification using search_emails (query: "receipt hold" or "receipt verification" or "suspicious stipend"). Discovery: (a) Marcus emailed Mina (email_marcus_to_mina_receipt_hold, subject: "Action needed, suspicious stipend receipt flagged for Kevin Tran (Vectral Systems)") instructing her to contact Rachel Whitfield at Vectral to verify Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard purchase, approach diplomatically as standard audit verification. (b) Marcus emailed Emeka (email_marcus_to_emeka_receipt_holds, subject: "Action needed, suspicious stipend receipts flagged for Andre Washington (Mosaic) and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop)") instructing him to contact Tamara Byrd at Mosaic Robotics about Andre Washington's $89 hold and Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop Analytics about Jordan Ekwueme's $45 hold, both as standard audit verifications.
OE 9: Search Linear for ExpenseBot-related engineering tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or "pilot" or "auto-categorizer") or linear_list_issues (project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer"). Discovery: (a) ENG-211 (issue_pilot_monitoring): "Pilot Monitoring, Vectral Systems & Mosaic Robotics", P1, in_progress, assigned to Samira Tariq. (b) ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config): "Fix Vectral Systems policy config", P1, in_progress. (c) ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed): "Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config", P1, in_progress. (d) ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses): "[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses", Urgent priority, in_progress, assigned to Dmitri Volkov. (e) ENG-219 (issue_launch_readiness_checklist): Launch readiness checklist noting $1,550+ exposure, 4 false rejections, 3 false approvals. (f) ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection): "Stipend duplicate detection, receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring", P1, to_do, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, labels include receipt-fraud, audit, pilot-incident. The agent should note that auto-decisions are currently disabled and fixes for both Vectral and Mosaic configs have been deployed, but the system has not relaunched.
OE 10: Search Airtable for affected stipend transaction records using airtable_list_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions") or airtable_search_records with field_name: "Account" and values "Vectral" or "Mosaic". Discovery of all employees affected by the ExpenseBot pilot:
Vectral Systems false rejections (pending manual correction):
- recStip000000018: Maria Santos, $550 ergonomic equipment, Rejected, pending manual correction (stale $400 default; Vectral policy allows $600)
- recStip000000019: James Okafor, $82 office supplies, Rejected, double bug, miscategorized AND stale limit, pending manual correction
- recStip000000020: Lisa Park, $130 internet equipment, Rejected, pending manual correction (stale $100 default; Vectral policy allows $150)
Vectral Systems false rejections (already corrected):
- recStip000000016: Danielle Osei, $475, Approved, initially auto-rejected, manually approved by Mina after Rachel Whitfield complaint
- recStip000000017: Kevin Tran, $185, Approved, initially auto-rejected, manually approved by Mina
Mosaic Robotics false approvals:
- recStip000000021: Andre Washington, $1,200 desk, Approved in error, exceeds $800 cap, string comparison bug
- recStip000000022: Andre Washington, $350 coworking, Approved in error, coworking excluded from Mosaic policy, excluded_categories config bug
- recStip000000023: Kenji Nakamura, $135 coworking day passes, Approved in error, same excluded_categories bug
Duplicate receipt cluster (separate from pilot issues):
- rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard: Kevin Tran, $89.00, Approved, flagged Duplicate Hash, identical SHA-256 to Andre Washington's receipt
- rec_stipend_andre_keyboard (Andre Washington, Mosaic) and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop), also flagged with audit markers
OE 11: Search CRM for expense-affected client accounts using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral") and crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") and crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop"). Discovery: (a) Vectral Systems: AM Mina Hashimoto, at-risk renewal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk, Rachel Whitfield demanded 15% discount citing ExpenseBot failures among other issues, MoveOps counter-offered 10% + Preferred Partner Program, Rachel's board met April 23 with no response yet). (b) Mosaic Robotics: AM Emeka Diallo, at-risk due to separate prototype damage incident (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk, $2,700). (c) BrightLoop Analytics: AM Mina Hashimoto, Q2 deal in progress (deal_brightloop_q2, $7,000, qualifiedToBuy). The agent should note that ExpenseBot issues have compounded existing relationship risks for Vectral and Mosaic.
OE 12: Post a support briefing in #customer-support using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C007", payload: briefing content covering both situations). The briefing should give Zara Kovacevic and Omar Ibrahim actionable information for handling inbound calls:
Terraform Digital section: Account background (Julian closed the deal, Mina Hashimoto is now AM after reply-all incident), 3 relocations pending for May (Priya Dasgupta May 5, Samantha Obi May 8, Marcus Liu May 12), Rachel Nguyen (rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com) may call since she considers Julian her primary contact, redirect relocation-specific questions to Mina (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com).
Expense auto-categorizer section: Pilot paused after configuration bugs caused false rejections at Vectral (Danielle Osei, Kevin Tran, Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) and false approvals at Mosaic (Andre Washington x2, Kenji Nakamura). Auto-decisions currently disabled. Duplicate receipt issue found across 3 clients. Per-employee status for anyone who may call:
- Maria Santos (Vectral): $550 claim still rejected, pending manual correction
- James Okafor (Vectral): $82 claim still rejected, pending manual correction
- Lisa Park (Vectral): $130 claim still rejected, pending manual correction
- Andre Washington (Mosaic): $89 keyboard on admin hold pending verification (STIP-2026-0094), also has $1,200 desk and $350 coworking approved in error, MoveOps absorbing cost per Elena's directive
- Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop): $45 monitor stand on admin hold pending verification (STIP-2026-0097)
- Kevin Tran (Vectral): $89 keyboard already paid, under audit review for duplicate receipt
- Kenji Nakamura (Mosaic): $135 coworking approved in error, MoveOps absorbing cost
HR contacts have been notified: Rachel Whitfield (Vectral), Tamara Byrd (Mosaic), Tessa Moreno (BrightLoop). If employees or HR call about frozen reimbursements, provide specific status and explain holds are part of a standard audit verification process.
OE 13: Update CRM notes on Terraform Digital account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: investigation findings, company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]). Content should include: Mina is AM of record, 3 relocations in qualification stage with no Airtable relocation records created yet (May 5/8/12 target dates), reply-all incident documented and relationship stabilized, AWS infrastructure concerns addressed via formal RCA (CloudWatch alarms being implemented), Rachel Nguyen remains primary client contact and trusts Julian as continuity POC, relocations are not on track and have been flagged to Mina.
OE 14: Update CRM notes on expense-affected accounts using crm_create_engagement for each account:
(a) Vectral Systems: ExpenseBot false rejections affected 5 employees (Danielle Osei and Kevin Tran corrected; Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park still pending manual correction). Kevin Tran also flagged for duplicate receipt ($89 already paid, under review). Mina contacted Rachel Whitfield for verification per Marcus's request. Note: this compounds the existing renewal risk, Rachel cited ExpenseBot failures in her discount demand.
(b) Mosaic Robotics: ExpenseBot false approvals for Andre Washington (2 claims totaling $1,550) and Kenji Nakamura ($135), MoveOps absorbing cost per Elena's directive. Andre Washington also flagged for duplicate receipt ($89 on admin hold, STIP-2026-0094). Emeka contacted Tamara Byrd for verification.
(c) BrightLoop Analytics: Jordan Ekwueme flagged for suspicious receipt ($45 on admin hold, STIP-2026-0097), same PDF template as duplicate cluster. BrightLoop was not part of the ExpenseBot pilot (pilot clients were Vectral and Mosaic only). Emeka contacted Tessa Moreno for verification.
OE 15: Send email to Rachel Nguyen from Julian using send_email (sender: "julian.brooks@moveops.com", recipients: ["rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com"], subject: relating to checking in ahead of the first relocations or similar). Content: Warm check-in ahead of the first relocation batch, letting Rachel know MoveOps is preparing for Priya, Marcus, and Samantha's moves, confirming Mina Hashimoto is her dedicated account manager for all relocation logistics, and noting that Julian remains available directly and she can reach him at any time if anything comes up. The email should NOT reference the pricing leak, internal AM drama, or any internal issues, it should be forward-looking, professional, and reassuring.
OE 16: Flag to Mina Hashimoto that Terraform relocations are not on track using send_email (sender: "julian.brooks@moveops.com", recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"]) or conversations_add_message. This is triggered by the discovery in OE 4 that no Airtable relocation records exist for any of the 3 Terraform employees despite Priya Dasgupta's May 5 move-in being only 9 days away. The flag should include: no relocation records created in Airtable, no coordinator assigned, no vendor contacts initiated, CRM deal still at qualification stage, Rachel has already provided detailed employee intake information (email-terraform-rachel-details-001) so Mina has what she needs to begin setup immediately.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Terraform Digital and Rachel Nguyen using search_emails (query: "Terraform" or "Rachel Nguyen" or "Vantage Distributed"). Read relevant threads using get_email_by_id or get_thread. Discovery: (a) Rachel Nguyen (rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com, VP of People Operations) sent the first relocation batch request to Julian (email-terraform-reloc-request-001), listing 3 employees: Priya Dasgupta (Sr. Data Engineer, Austin TX → Chicago IL, move-in first week of May / May 5), Marcus Liu (Product Designer, Portland OR → New York NY, move-in around May 12), and Samantha Obi (DevOps Engineer, Denver CO → San Francisco CA, move-in May 8, must be at SF office by May 11). (b) Julian forwarded to David Chen with Rachel CC'd, calling Rachel "my primary contact" (email-terraform-reloc-fwd-001). (c) Internal pricing discussion followed among David, Marcus Thorne, and Alejandro about margin floor and tiered discounts. (d) Catalina Dubois accidentally Reply-All'd the internal pricing thread to Rachel (email-terraform-replyall-001), exposing MoveOps' 25% margin floor and tiered discount structure. (e) Rachel acknowledged the leak professionally but firmly, noted Julian had been her "only point of contact since we signed" (email-terraform-rachel-response-001). (f) Catalina sent an apology email confirming Elena assigned Mina Hashimoto as dedicated AM and Julian would continue to be available (email-terraform-apology-001). (g) Mina sent introduction email to Rachel restating the 3 relocations, positioning herself as dedicated AM for relocation services (email-terraform-mina-intro-001). (h) Rachel replied to Mina with detailed intake information for the employees including Priya's cat requiring pet-friendly housing (email-terraform-rachel-details-001), with Julian CC'd.
OE 2: Search Slack for Terraform and Vantage Distributed discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Vantage Distributed" or "Vantage"). Read relevant threads using conversations_replies. Discovery: (a) Julian announced the Vantage Distributed deal in #customer-engagement (C002), $1,850,000, 1,000+ employees. (b) Catalina set up portal admin accounts for Rachel Nguyen, Derek Liu, and Sarah Chen. (c) Julian tied the AWS cost spike to the Vantage Distributed onboarding traffic in #root-cause-aws-spike (C009), admitting he should have advocated more for infrastructure cost review before finalizing pricing. (d) All three AMs declined Terraform account ownership, Mina cited Vectral crisis, Catalina cited NorthWind, Emeka cited Mosaic damage claim, before Elena assigned to Mina. (e) Julian later stated in #customer-engagement: "Rachel sent her request to me because I'm the person she trusts" and expressed regret about not staying as POC.
OE 3: Search CRM for Terraform Digital / Vantage Distributed using crm_search_companies (name: "Vantage Distributed") or crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform"). Find company record (company-terraform-digital, name: "Vantage Distributed", domain: vantagedistributed.com, 1,200 employees). Then search for deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company-terraform-digital"). Discovery: (a) deal-terraform-digital-001: "Vantage Distributed - Enterprise Relocation Package", $1,850,000, closedWon, closed 2026-03-18. (b) deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1: "Terraform Digital, Q2 2026 Relocation Batch 1 (3 employees)", $7,500, qualification stage, created 2026-04-23. Description lists Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi with routes and May dates, AM: Mina Hashimoto, dedicated coordinator authorized. Then check engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]). Discovery: engagement-terraform-replyall-incident documenting the full Reply-All incident timeline, exposed pricing details, resolution actions, and current status ("Relationship stabilized. Rachel is engaged and providing relocation details. Monitoring closely").
OE 4: Search Airtable for Terraform relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Terraform"). Also try value: "Vantage". Discovery: NO relocation records exist in the Relocations table (tblRelocations01) for any of the 3 Terraform employees. The relocations have not been set up. A separate search of the Client Accounts table (tblClientAccts01) finds recTerraformDigital01 showing Client: "Terraform Digital", Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto, Status: Onboarding, Total Relocations: 3, Notes documenting the Reply-All incident and that "Previous account contact: Julian Brooks... Mina is AM of record." This confirms the relocations are NOT on track, no coordinator assigned, no vendor contacts, no housing or flights arranged, and Priya Dasgupta's May 5 move-in is only 9 days away from the universe date of April 26.
OE 5: Search emails and Slack for AWS infrastructure resolution using search_emails (query: "AWS" or "cost spike" or "infrastructure") and/or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "AWS" or "cost spike", filter_in_channel: "C009"). Discovery: Samira Tariq published a formal RCA identifying the root cause as Vantage Distributed onboarding traffic, self-serve portal hit by ~1,200 users caused autoscaling from 4 to 18 instances, 3x cost increase to approximately $42,870 vs. $14,300 baseline. Resolution: CloudWatch alarms being implemented, pricing model adjustments underway, Elena directed the team to add cost alerts. The infrastructure concern has been identified and addressed with an RCA, but the agent should note whether cost protections are fully deployed for the upcoming relocation wave.
OE 6: Search emails for ExpenseBot / expense auto-categorizer pilot using search_emails (query: "expense auto-categorizer" or "pilot" or "auto-approve" or "auto-reject"). Read relevant threads using get_email_by_id or get_thread. Discovery: (a) Samira Tariq launched the auto-categorizer pilot with Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics as pilot clients. (b) Vectral experienced false rejections: legitimate expenses rejected due to stale default policy limits (e.g., ergonomic cap showed $400 instead of correct $600, internet cap showed $100 instead of $150). Mina escalated after Rachel Whitfield complained about Danielle Osei's $475 chair rejection. (c) Mosaic experienced false approvals: out-of-policy expenses approved due to malformed JSON config, excluded_categories stored as string instead of array (exclusion checks skipped), max_amount stored as string causing broken comparisons. Emeka reported Andre Washington's $1,200 desk (over $800 cap) and $350/month coworking (excluded category) approved in error, approximately $1,550 exposure. (d) Elena issued a pause directive (email_elena_directive): suspend auto-approve and auto-deny, keep auto-categorize with human review, order full audit. (e) David Chen escalated client relationship impact citing Vectral trust erosion and Mosaic reconciliation risk. (f) Marcus Thorne raised financial concerns and requested full audit halt on auto-approvals.
OE 7: Search emails for duplicate receipt and stipend fraud findings using search_emails (query: "duplicate receipt" or "stipend audit" or "TechComfort" or "SHA-256"). Discovery: Dmitri Volkov emailed Marcus Thorne (email_dmitri_receipt_audit_findings, subject: "Stipend audit finding, duplicate receipt anomalies across 3 clients (Vectral, Mosaic, BrightLoop)") with three flagged transactions: (1) Kevin Tran (Vectral Systems), $89.00 ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, STIP-2026-0089, already PAID on 2026-03-08. (2) Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics), $89.00 identical keyboard from TechComfort Supply, STIP-2026-0094, Pending, byte-for-byte identical SHA-256 hash to Tran's receipt. (3) Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop Analytics), $45.00 monitor stand from TechComfort Supply, STIP-2026-0097, Pending, different hash but same PDF template metadata (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). Total exposure: $223.00 ($89 paid + $134 pending). Marcus replied (email_marcus_qb_status_reply) confirming he placed administrative holds on both pending payments (STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097). Note: BrightLoop Analytics was not one of the two pilot clients (Vectral and Mosaic), so the duplicate receipt issue spans beyond the pilot scope.
OE 8: Search emails for AM communications to HR contacts about receipt verification using search_emails (query: "receipt hold" or "receipt verification" or "suspicious stipend"). Discovery: (a) Marcus emailed Mina (email_marcus_to_mina_receipt_hold, subject: "Action needed, suspicious stipend receipt flagged for Kevin Tran (Vectral Systems)") instructing her to contact Rachel Whitfield at Vectral to verify Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard purchase, approach diplomatically as standard audit verification. (b) Marcus emailed Emeka (email_marcus_to_emeka_receipt_holds, subject: "Action needed, suspicious stipend receipts flagged for Andre Washington (Mosaic) and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop)") instructing him to contact Tamara Byrd at Mosaic Robotics about Andre Washington's $89 hold and Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop Analytics about Jordan Ekwueme's $45 hold, both as standard audit verifications.
OE 9: Search Linear for ExpenseBot-related engineering tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or "pilot" or "auto-categorizer") or linear_list_issues (project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer"). Discovery: (a) ENG-211 (issue_pilot_monitoring): "Pilot Monitoring, Vectral Systems & Mosaic Robotics", P1, in_progress, assigned to Samira Tariq. (b) ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config): "Fix Vectral Systems policy config", P1, in_progress. (c) ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed): "Fix Mosaic Robotics policy config", P1, in_progress. (d) ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses): "[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses", Urgent priority, in_progress, assigned to Dmitri Volkov. (e) ENG-219 (issue_launch_readiness_checklist): Launch readiness checklist noting $1,550+ exposure, 4 false rejections, 3 false approvals. (f) ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection): "Stipend duplicate detection, receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring", P1, to_do, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, labels include receipt-fraud, audit, pilot-incident. The agent should note that auto-decisions are currently disabled and fixes for both Vectral and Mosaic configs have been deployed, but the system has not relaunched.
OE 10: Search Airtable for affected stipend transaction records using airtable_list_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions") or airtable_search_records with field_name: "Account" and values "Vectral" or "Mosaic". Discovery of all employees affected by the ExpenseBot pilot:
Vectral Systems false rejections (pending manual correction):
- recStip000000018: Maria Santos, $550 ergonomic equipment, Rejected, pending manual correction (stale $400 default; Vectral policy allows $600)
- recStip000000019: James Okafor, $82 office supplies, Rejected, double bug, miscategorized AND stale limit, pending manual correction
- recStip000000020: Lisa Park, $130 internet equipment, Rejected, pending manual correction (stale $100 default; Vectral policy allows $150)
Vectral Systems false rejections (already corrected):
- recStip000000016: Danielle Osei, $475, Approved, initially auto-rejected, manually approved by Mina after Rachel Whitfield complaint
- recStip000000017: Kevin Tran, $185, Approved, initially auto-rejected, manually approved by Mina
Mosaic Robotics false approvals:
- recStip000000021: Andre Washington, $1,200 desk, Approved in error, exceeds $800 cap, string comparison bug
- recStip000000022: Andre Washington, $350 coworking, Approved in error, coworking excluded from Mosaic policy, excluded_categories config bug
- recStip000000023: Kenji Nakamura, $135 coworking day passes, Approved in error, same excluded_categories bug
Duplicate receipt cluster (separate from pilot issues):
- rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard: Kevin Tran, $89.00, Approved, flagged Duplicate Hash, identical SHA-256 to Andre Washington's receipt
- rec_stipend_andre_keyboard (Andre Washington, Mosaic) and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop), also flagged with audit markers
OE 11: Search CRM for expense-affected client accounts using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral") and crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") and crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop"). Discovery: (a) Vectral Systems: AM Mina Hashimoto, at-risk renewal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk, Rachel Whitfield demanded 15% discount citing ExpenseBot failures among other issues, MoveOps counter-offered 10% + Preferred Partner Program, Rachel's board met April 23 with no response yet). (b) Mosaic Robotics: AM Emeka Diallo, at-risk due to separate prototype damage incident (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk, $2,700). (c) BrightLoop Analytics: AM Mina Hashimoto, Q2 deal in progress (deal_brightloop_q2, $7,000, qualifiedToBuy). The agent should note that ExpenseBot issues have compounded existing relationship risks for Vectral and Mosaic.
OE 12: Post a support briefing in #customer-support using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C007", payload: briefing content covering both situations). The briefing should give Zara Kovacevic and Omar Ibrahim actionable information for handling inbound calls:
Terraform Digital section: Account background (Julian closed the deal, Mina Hashimoto is now AM after reply-all incident), 3 relocations pending for May (Priya Dasgupta May 5, Samantha Obi May 8, Marcus Liu May 12), Rachel Nguyen (rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com) may call since she considers Julian her primary contact, redirect relocation-specific questions to Mina (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com).
Expense auto-categorizer section: Pilot paused after configuration bugs caused false rejections at Vectral (Danielle Osei, Kevin Tran, Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park) and false approvals at Mosaic (Andre Washington x2, Kenji Nakamura). Auto-decisions currently disabled. Duplicate receipt issue found across 3 clients. Per-employee status for anyone who may call:
- Maria Santos (Vectral): $550 claim still rejected, pending manual correction
- James Okafor (Vectral): $82 claim still rejected, pending manual correction
- Lisa Park (Vectral): $130 claim still rejected, pending manual correction
- Andre Washington (Mosaic): $89 keyboard on admin hold pending verification (STIP-2026-0094), also has $1,200 desk and $350 coworking approved in error, MoveOps absorbing cost per Elena's directive
- Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop): $45 monitor stand on admin hold pending verification (STIP-2026-0097)
- Kevin Tran (Vectral): $89 keyboard already paid, under audit review for duplicate receipt
- Kenji Nakamura (Mosaic): $135 coworking approved in error, MoveOps absorbing cost
HR contacts have been notified: Rachel Whitfield (Vectral), Tamara Byrd (Mosaic), Tessa Moreno (BrightLoop). If employees or HR call about frozen reimbursements, provide specific status and explain holds are part of a standard audit verification process.
OE 13: Update CRM notes on Terraform Digital account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: investigation findings, company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]). Content should include: Mina is AM of record, 3 relocations in qualification stage with no Airtable relocation records created yet (May 5/8/12 target dates), reply-all incident documented and relationship stabilized, AWS infrastructure concerns addressed via formal RCA (CloudWatch alarms being implemented), Rachel Nguyen remains primary client contact and trusts Julian as continuity POC, relocations are not on track and have been flagged to Mina.
OE 14: Update CRM notes on expense-affected accounts using crm_create_engagement for each account:
(a) Vectral Systems: ExpenseBot false rejections affected 5 employees (Danielle Osei and Kevin Tran corrected; Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park still pending manual correction). Kevin Tran also flagged for duplicate receipt ($89 already paid, under review). Mina contacted Rachel Whitfield for verification per Marcus's request. Note: this compounds the existing renewal risk, Rachel cited ExpenseBot failures in her discount demand.
(b) Mosaic Robotics: ExpenseBot false approvals for Andre Washington (2 claims totaling $1,550) and Kenji Nakamura ($135), MoveOps absorbing cost per Elena's directive. Andre Washington also flagged for duplicate receipt ($89 on admin hold, STIP-2026-0094). Emeka contacted Tamara Byrd for verification.
(c) BrightLoop Analytics: Jordan Ekwueme flagged for suspicious receipt ($45 on admin hold, STIP-2026-0097), same PDF template as duplicate cluster. BrightLoop was not part of the ExpenseBot pilot (pilot clients were Vectral and Mosaic only). Emeka contacted Tessa Moreno for verification.
OE 15: Send email to Rachel Nguyen from Julian using send_email (sender: "julian.brooks@moveops.com", recipients: ["rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com"], subject: relating to checking in ahead of the first relocations or similar). Content: Warm check-in ahead of the first relocation batch, letting Rachel know MoveOps is preparing for Priya, Marcus, and Samantha's moves, confirming Mina Hashimoto is her dedicated account manager for all relocation logistics, and noting that Julian remains available directly and she can reach him at any time if anything comes up. The email should NOT reference the pricing leak, internal AM drama, or any internal issues, it should be forward-looking, professional, and reassuring.
OE 16: Flag to Mina Hashimoto that Terraform relocations are not on track using send_email (sender: "julian.brooks@moveops.com", recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"]) or conversations_add_message. This is triggered by the discovery in OE 4 that no Airtable relocation records exist for any of the 3 Terraform employees despite Priya Dasgupta's May 5 move-in being only 9 days away. The flag should include: no relocation records created in Airtable, no coordinator assigned, no vendor contacts initiated, CRM deal still at qualification stage, Rachel has already provided detailed employee intake information (email-terraform-rachel-details-001) so Mina has what she needs to begin setup immediately.

21 The final response addresses the pricing leak aftermath, noting that the reply-all incident has been documented and the client relationship has stabilized (Rachel Nguyen is engaged and providing relocation details to Mina), and/or that Rachel has locked in the volume discount tiers from the leaked email (8% at 11-20 relocations and 12% at 21+, or similar), and/or that an invoice email safety check to prevent future leaks was never built (or similar loose-end finding).
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓—— 50%
✓Run #1The final response notes 'The pricing situation from the leak is actually stable — Rachel accepted the tiered structure, first batch at standard rates ($2,500/move), volume tiers for future batches. That's resolved.' This covers relationship stabilization and Rachel locking in discount tiers, satisfying the OR-criteria.
✗Run #2The final response does not explicitly address loose ends from the pricing leak such as whether Rachel has locked in discount tiers seen in the leaked email, whether the invoice attachment safety ticket was implemented, or other specific unresolved issues stemming from the incident. The summary lists the apology and pricing framework as complete but does not discuss loose-end risks.
✓Run #3The final response describes the pricing leak aftermath: Rachel 'handled it with pointed grace' and provided her relocation specs to Mina (relationship engaged), Elena 'directed honoring the exposed pricing tiers' (Rachel locked in discount tiers), and the incident context is documented. The criterion's 'and/or' conditions are satisfied.
✓Run #4The final response addresses the pricing leak aftermath, noting the recovery was 'well-executed,' the relationship is 'stabilized,' Rachel is 'engaged and cooperative,' and that Elena decided to honor the tiered pricing Rachel saw. The CRM note also references that Rachel is aware of tiered pricing from the leak and notes what can/cannot be proactively offered. This satisfies the 'and/or' criterion.
—Run #5The final response mentions that the pricing leak crisis 'was handled well,' that 'the relationship is stabilized but fragile,' and that Elena directed honoring the tiered pricing Rachel saw. This addresses the relationship stabilization aspect. However, the loose end of the invoice attachment safety check (linear ticket in backlog) that could lead to future leaks is not identified or mentioned.
—Run #6The final response briefly states 'The pricing leak is resolved — Rachel accepted the tiered structure, Catalina apologized, Mina is AM of record' and notes Rachel is 'watching closely.' This covers the stabilized relationship and Rachel locking in the tiers, but does not mention the invoice attachment safety check still in backlog (linear ticket) as a remaining loose end, which the criterion specifically identifies as a potential gap.
